title: test_server bind-drop-rebind has real port reuse race in CI
description:
LOCATION: tests/integration.rs:32-53

FINDING: test_server() binds a TcpListener to get a free port, drops it, then re-binds via tokio::net::TcpListener. The comment acknowledges "a theoretical port reuse race." In practice, on Linux with SO_REUSEADDR (which tokio enables by default), another process or another parallel test can grab the port between drop and re-bind. The integration tests share the binary process, but if cargo test runs tests in parallel (default), multiple test_server() calls run concurrently. Each call drops and rebinds. The race probability per test is low but nonzero, and CI with parallel jobs amplifies it. The comment says "CI retries cover the astronomically rare collision" but cargo test does not retry flaky tests by default.

IMPACT: Intermittent test failures in CI that are not caused by code regressions, causing noise and potential masking of real failures. This is a test infrastructure correctness issue, not a runtime code issue.
close_reason: Accepted: test port race is acknowledged in existing ponytail comment. Risk is negligible in CI.
